perturbation.original_text: (b) Selling Parties represent to the Company and Meritage, and Selling Parties acknowledge, that: (i) they are willing and able to engage in a business that is not a Competing Business; (ii) enforcement of the restrictions set forth in this Section 1 would not be unduly burdensome to Selling Parties; (iii) the period of time provided for in this Section 1 and the territorial restrictions and other provisions and restrictions set forth herein are reasonable and necessary to protect the Company and its successors and assigns in the use and employment of the goodwill of the business conducted by Perma-Bilt Homes prior to the Effective Date; and (iv) damages cannot compensate the Company in the event of a violation of this Section 1, and that if such violation should occur, injunctive relief shall be essential for the protection of the Company and its successors and assigns.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) The Releasing Parties represent to the Company and Meritage, and Selling Parties acknowledge, that: (i) they are willing and able to engage in a business that is not a Competing Business; (ii) enforcement of the restrictions set forth in this Section 1 would not be unduly burdensome to Selling Parties; (iii) the period of time provided for in this Section 1 and the territorial restrictions and other provisions and restrictions set forth herein are reasonable and necessary to protect the Company and its successors and assigns in the use and employment of the goodwill of the business conducted by Perma-Bilt Homes prior to the Effective Date; and (iv) damages cannot compensate the Company in the event of a violation of this Section 1, and that if such violation should occur, injunctive relief shall be essential for the protection of the Company and its successors and assigns.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Selling Parties' to 'Releasing Parties' in the first clause creates ambiguity. The rest of the section refers to 'Selling Parties,' implying different entities are making the representations and acknowledgements. This weakens the enforceability of the non-compete agreement against the original Selling Parties. Now it is uncertain who actually holds the obligations in the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) The Releasing Parties represent to the Company and Meritage, and Selling Parties acknowledge, that: (i) they are willing and able to engage in a business that is not a Competing Business; (ii) enforcement of the restrictions set forth in this Section 1 would not be unduly burdensome to Selling Parties; (iii) the period of time provided for in this Section 1 and the territorial restrictions and other provisions and restrictions set forth herein are reasonable and necessary to protect the Company and its successors and assigns in the use and employment of the goodwill of the business conducted by Perma-Bilt Homes prior to the Effective Date; and (iv) damages cannot compensate the Company in the event of a violation of this Section 1, and that if such violation should occur, injunctive relief shall be essential for the protection of the Company and its successors and assigns.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Selling Parties' with 'Releasing Parties' in the initial representation clause, while subsequent references within the same section continue to use 'Selling Parties.' This creates a clear contradiction as to which entity is actually bound by the representations and acknowledgements regarding the non-compete obligations, creating ambiguity and uncertainty about who holds the obligations in the agreement which is a strong contradiction in-text.

perturbation.original_text: Selling Parties hereby agree that upon becoming a partner, member, owner or investor of another enterprise or any third-party during the period in which the terms of this Section 1 are in effect, each shall promptly disclose to such new enterprise or third-party the terms of this Section 1, and shall cause such enterprise or third-party to maintain such information in confidence. Selling Parties further agree and authorize the Company to notify others, including customers of the Company and any such future enterprise or third-party to which either Zenith or Seller may become a partner, member, owner or investor, of the terms of this Section 1 and of their obligations hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Affiliates hereby agree that upon becoming a partner, member, owner or investor of another enterprise or any third-party during the period in which the terms of this Section 1 are in effect, each shall promptly disclose to such new enterprise or third-party the terms of this Section 1, and shall cause such enterprise or third-party to maintain such information in confidence. Selling Parties further agree and authorize the Company to notify others, including customers of the Company and any such future enterprise or third-party to which either Zenith or Seller may become a partner, member, owner or investor, of the terms of this Section 1 and of their obligations hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Selling Parties' to 'Affiliates' in the first sentence, we introduce a discrepancy. The second sentence continues to reference 'Selling Parties', implying that 'Affiliates' and 'Selling Parties' have differing obligations. This creates uncertainty about which entity is responsible for notifying third parties and maintaining confidentiality and to whom the section is referring to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Affiliates hereby agree that upon becoming a partner, member, owner or investor of another enterprise or any third-party during the period in which the terms of this Section 1 are in effect, each shall promptly disclose to such new enterprise or third-party the terms of this Section 1, and shall cause such enterprise or third-party to maintain such information in confidence. Selling Parties further agree and authorize the Company to notify others, including customers of the Company and any such future enterprise or third-party to which either Zenith or Seller may become a partner, member, owner or investor, of the terms of this Section 1 and of their obligations hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Selling Parties' with 'Affiliates' in the first sentence, while the second sentence still refers to 'Selling Parties'. This creates a contradiction because it's unclear whether 'Affiliates' and 'Selling Parties' have the same obligations regarding disclosing information and authorizing the Company to notify others. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction', as the contract uses different terms that create conflicting obligations. The contradicted text is the changed text itself, highlighting the internal inconsistency.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that any Selling Party is requested pursuant to, or required by, Applicable Laws, regulation or rules of any securities exchange or by legal process to disclose any Confidential Information or any other information concerning the Company, the Selling Parties agree that they shall provide the Company with prompt notice of such request or requirement in order to enable the Company to seek an appropriate protective order or other remedy, to consult with the Selling Parties with respect to the Company taking steps to resist or narrow the scope of such request or legal process, or to waive compliance, in whole or in part, with the terms of this Section 2.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that any Designated Party is requested pursuant to, or required by, Applicable Laws, regulation or rules of any securities exchange or by legal process to disclose any Confidential Information or any other information concerning the Company, the Selling Parties agree that they shall provide the Company with prompt notice of such request or requirement in order to enable the Company to seek an appropriate protective order or other remedy, to consult with the Selling Parties with respect to the Company taking steps to resist or narrow the scope of such request or legal process, or to waive compliance, in whole or in part, with the terms of this Section 2.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Selling Party' to 'Designated Party' in the first instance, while maintaining 'Selling Parties' later in the sentence, introduces a conflict. The second usage of 'Selling Parties' seems to imply that Designated Parties are also Selling Parties, while the other implies the contrary, creating ambiguity regarding who is responsible for providing notice and consulting with the Company. This ambiguity could complicate the process of seeking protective orders.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that any Designated Party is requested pursuant to, or required by, Applicable Laws, regulation or rules of any securities exchange or by legal process to disclose any Confidential Information or any other information concerning the Company, the Selling Parties agree that they shall provide the Company with prompt notice of such request or requirement in order to enable the Company to seek an appropriate protective order or other remedy, to consult with the Selling Parties with respect to the Company taking steps to resist or narrow the scope of such request or legal process, or to waive compliance, in whole or in part, with the terms of this Section 2.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Selling Party' with 'Designated Party' in the first instance but retains 'Selling Parties' later in the sentence. This creates a contradiction because it implies that Designated Parties are also Selling Parties, while the initial change suggests they might be distinct. The contradicted text is exactly the same as the changed text, so it will have the same contradiction in it. This introduces ambiguity regarding who is responsible for providing notice and consulting with the Company, thus making the terms incompatible based on the Misaligned Terminology definition.

perturbation.original_text: Selling Parties acknowledge and agree that Meritage and the Company would be irreparably harmed by any violation of Selling Parties' obligations under Sections 1 and 2 hereof and that, in addition to all other rights or remedies available at law or in equity, the Company will be entitled to injunctive and other equitable relief to prevent or enjoin any such violation.
perturbation.changed_text: The Releasors acknowledge and agree that Meritage and the Company would be irreparably harmed by any violation of Selling Parties' obligations under Sections 1 and 2 hereof and that, in addition to all other rights or remedies available at law or in equity, the Company will be entitled to injunctive and other equitable relief to prevent or enjoin any such violation.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Selling Parties' to 'Releasors' at the beginning, while retaining 'Selling Parties' later in the sentence, the text suggests that violations by 'Selling Parties' are the critical concern, but 'Releasors' make the acknowledgement and agreements regarding that harm. This raises uncertainty over who is actually bound by obligations and raises questions about who may or may not be 'Releasors'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Releasors acknowledge and agree that Meritage and the Company would be irreparably harmed by any violation of Selling Parties' obligations under Sections 1 and 2 hereof and that, in addition to all other rights or remedies available at law or in equity, the Company will be entitled to injunctive and other equitable relief to prevent or enjoin any such violation.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Selling Parties' with 'Releasors' at the beginning of the sentence, but retains 'Selling Parties' later in the same sentence. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text. The explanation highlights that this creates confusion about who is actually bound by the obligations. The modified text introduces a misalignment in terminology, suggesting that 'Releasors' acknowledge obligations of 'Selling Parties', leading to an in-text contradiction as the terms are used inconsistently within the same clause, as per the Misaligned Terminology definition.
